name: Potato Rolls

ingredients.item: potato, cooked and mashed (from about 1 large russet

method: Combine 1 teaspoon sugar and lukewarm water in a glass
measuring cup or small dish. Sprinkle yeast on top and set aside
about 5 minutes, or until frothy. In a small bowl, dissolve yeast
in water.
Combine flour, remaining tablespoon sugar, and salt in the bowl
of a stand mixer fitted with the dough hook. Next, combine the
yeast mixture and the potatoes in a mixing bowl. While the
mixer is running, add the butter a tablespoon until it is
completely incorporated. Continue mixing for another 5
minutes, or until a smooth dough develops.
Spray a large mixing bowl with cooking spray and place the
dough in it. Cover with a kitchen towel and rise for 1 hour, or
until doubled in size.
Deflate the dough by punching it down, then turn it out onto a
lightly floured surface. Make 12 equal portions of dough and
roll each into a smooth ball.
Spray a 9" x13" baking sheet with cooking spray and arrange
the balls in it, leaving an inch between them. Cover and set
aside for another 45 minutes to rise.
Preheat oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit (200 degrees Celsius)
(200 degrees Celsius).Using melted butter, brush the tops of the
dough—Bake for about 25 minutes, or until brown.
Drizzle with extra melted butter and serve while still warm.
